title: thermopsis montana var. ovata
intro: Thermopsis montana var. ovata, also known as Mountain Lupine, is a perennial plant belonging to the legume family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and distinctive, fern-like foliage. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The Mountain Lupine has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in traditional folk remedies, highlighting its versatility in alternative medicine.
